qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: REST project-user mutation endpoints let managers self-promote and remove higher-level members
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a REST authorization flaw in `api/rest/restcore/projects_rest.php`, `ProjectUsersAddCommand.php`, and `ProjectUsersDeleteCommand.php`; the advisory is stored HTML injection from unescaped historical tag names in timeline rendering for `my_view_page.php`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a stored HTML injection/XSS caused by `IssueTagTimelineEvent::html()` rendering a historical tag name without `string_html_specialchars()` when the tag was renamed or deleted, while the session's manifest-backed qualifying finding is a separate MantisBT REST project-user ACL bug.
evidence: The advisory patch `f32787c14d4518476fe7f05f992dbfe6eaccd815` changes only `core/classes/IssueTagTimelineEvent.class.php`, replacing the raw fallback `$this->tag_name` with `string_html_specialchars( $this->tag_name )` in timeline HTML generation.

`rest_project_user_acl_privilege_escalation/vulnerability_report.json` identifies missing actor-vs-requested-access and actor-vs-target rank checks in REST project-user mutation handlers (`api/rest/restcore/projects_rest.php`, `ProjectUsersAddCommand.php`, `ProjectUsersDeleteCommand.php`), not tag or timeline rendering.

`artifacts/component_family_checklist.md` clears the export/output rendering family and accepts the REST/API authorization family instead, which is consistent with the qualifying findings not matching the target XSS root cause.
